notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.
notes.contentHtml:
This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the "Recommended Preparation" section below to support timely degree completion.


Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: Although not required for admission, prospective transfer students interested in this major are encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of the following THREE course requirements prior to transfer: ONE lower-division core course in Jewish history or literature (section A below) ONE lower-division Holocaust core course (section B below) ONE lower-division elective (section C below)* *May be an additional core course Refer to the " Recommended Preparation " section below for courses approved to articulate to these requirements. Language Requirement The Jewish Studies B.A. requires three quarters of lower-division instruction (or equivalent) in a Jewish language. Transfer students with prior knowledge of Hebrew or Yiddish are advised to complete a language placement exam to determine if they have satisfied any of the elementary language course requirements. Additional Information for Transfer Transfer students entering at the junior level can readily finish all of the Jewish Studies major requirements within two years, even if they haven’t completed any preparatory coursework prior to transferring. Some background in history and in literary analysis and interpretation is desirable, but not essential. Since this is an interdisciplinary major, preparatory work in art history, philosophy, or politics, depending on the student’s interests, may also be useful. Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Jewish Studies website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Jewish Studies B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Although not required for admission, prospective transfer students interested in this major are encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of the following THREE course requirements prior to transfer:


  • ONE lower-division core course in Jewish history or literature (section A below)
  • ONE lower-division Holocaust core course (section B below)
  • ONE lower-division elective (section C below)*

*May be an additional core course


Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for courses approved to articulate to these requirements.



Language Requirement

The Jewish Studies B.A. requires three quarters of lower-division instruction (or equivalent) in a Jewish language. Transfer students with prior knowledge of Hebrew or Yiddish are advised to complete a language placement exam to determine if they have satisfied any of the elementary language course requirements.



Additional Information for Transfer

Transfer students entering at the junior level can readily finish all of the Jewish Studies major requirements within two years, even if they haven’t completed any preparatory coursework prior to transferring. Some background in history and in literary analysis and interpretation is desirable, but not essential. Since this is an interdisciplinary major, preparatory work in art history, philosophy, or politics, depending on the student’s interests, may also be useful.


Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Visit the Jewish Studies website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Jewish Studies B.A. Program Statement
